title: Glandora nitida
intro: Glandora nitida, also known as Cat's Tail or Purple Cat's Tail, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Glandora nitida include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Glandora nitida is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from menstrual cramps.
taxonomy: Glandora nitida is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae. This family is commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. The genus Glandora is closely related to the genus Heliotropium, with which it shares similar characteristics. The Boraginaceae family consists of around 2,000 species, many of which are found in the Mediterranean region. Glandora nitida is a native species to this region, specifically the Iberian Peninsula.
